title: leontodon pyrenaicus subsp. cantabricus
intro: Leontodon pyrenaicus subsp. cantabricus, commonly known as the Cantabrian hawkbit, is a subspecies of the Leontodon pyrenaicus plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. The Cantabrian hawkbit has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant's medicinal properties have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds, which can provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the Cantabrian hawkbit is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also employed in traditional folk medicine in the Cantabrian region of Spain.
